behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing, and missing fields return one clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: the failure boundary is the handoff between validation and downstream pipeline execution (must assert downstream modules are not invoked when required fields are missing). Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires pre-processing validation/error aggregation, not real OS/network/subprocess behavior; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake orchestrator with real validator + downstream service fakes/spies; submit payloads missing multiple required fields and assert a single error contains all missing field names and that insurance/triage/scheduling are not called.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is pure parsing/range logic inside one validation boundary. Rejection gate: behavior text is only input validation rules; no module handoff is required (so not Integration), and no OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency exists (so not System/Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Directly test DOB validator function/object with table-driven cases: valid date, future date, >150 years old, malformed strings.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending responses.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: failure occurs at module/interface handoff between eligibility service and API client payload/response mapping. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text focuses on request fields and status interpretation, which can be validated with an in-process test double; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because payer API responses are deterministic status values, not model-quality dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Run service + real serialization/client wiring against a local stub endpoint/fake transport; assert outgoing request contains exact patient ID/plan code and each of three response statuses maps to correct internal eligibility state.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues instead of blocking.
behaviors.justification: Integration -> System proof: the behavior explicitly targets real unreachability (connection refused/timeout/DNS/network path), which depends on real HTTP/network error modes and can be hidden by simplistic mocks. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI-model correctness is part of this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute intake against a real HTTP target made unreachable (e.g., blackholed host/closed port/forced timeout); assert eligibility becomes pending and pipeline proceeds to later steps without hard failure.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: System -> Agentic proof: correctness depends on real model output quality/classification behavior for symptom semantics; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide misclassification failures. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified because behavior describes one agentic decision step, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative emergency symptom inputs through the real triage model endpoint and assert urgency is high with acceptance thresholds over repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Symptom triage returns a clinician-reviewable natural-language explanation, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: System -> Agentic proof: 'clinician can review' implies explanation quality/readability from model generation; a stubbed string would mask real failures (empty, incoherent, non-clinical text). Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified because this is still a single model output step, not multi-agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke real triage generation for varied symptom inputs and evaluate that explanation text is present, human-readable, and semantically tied to the urgency rationale (not just numeric/token placeholders).

behaviors.description: Scheduler picks the first available slot matching urgency policy: high same-day, medium within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: failure appears at handoff between urgency assignment, availability query, and calendar persistence ordering logic across modules/DB access. Rejection gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is policy + slot selection and can be validated with local DB/module wiring; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Use scheduling service wired to a local calendar DB fixture with controlled slots; assert selected slot is earliest that satisfies urgency SLA windows for high and medium cases.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book a slot under concurrent processing of two patients.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: failure is a cross-module/state race at scheduler + shared calendar DB transaction boundary under concurrency. Integration-brake check: no required real external network/subprocess beyond local DB/concurrency primitives, no AI dependency, no multi-agentic chain; therefore Integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests against the same local calendar DB slot using real transaction/locking logic; assert only one booking succeeds and the other is rerouted/fails cleanly without duplicate reservation.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-appropriate preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic message composition/content selection logic. Rejection gate: behavior text only requires correct field inclusion and instruction selection; no mandatory module boundary, real infra, or AI quality dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Test confirmation renderer/composer with fixture inputs for visit types and assert required fields and correct preparation instructions are present.

behaviors.description: End-to-end intake pipeline (validate -> insurance -> triage -> schedule -> confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: full pipeline spans multiple module handoffs. Integration -> System proof: includes real external payer API call behavior in pipeline execution. System -> Agentic proof: pipeline includes real AI triage step whose non-deterministic output/format can cause real completion failures that stubs hide. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified because only one agentic step is specified (triage), not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end submission in a production-like environment using real payer API integration path and real triage model, with scheduling DB and confirmation delivery enabled; assert pipeline reaches completed state automatically and artifacts from each stage are produced.
